what_it_does: This file is a posted noticeboard note authored by tim in the dragnet-development channel; it comments on item-1492565a and records Tim's remarks about a target-agnostic annotation pipeline and about prior prototype projects (vi-visual, Obsidian, and others) that he says can only be made useful through a dragnet/graph-execution composition process.
observations: It is a note with source claude_code, state posted, and a commented_on link to board://item-1492565a.
It affirms an implied pipeline (selection→envelope→submit→route→reply) that must be target-agnostic, treating any 'commentable thing' as an addressable node (board:// or ui://).
It distinguishes the vi-visual project/repo (vi-visual-bridge) from vi-visual-dev and notes it used markdown and Supabase.
It describes the prototype codebases as accumulated, inconsistent, tangled, and 'illegal and invalid by default' for production use.
It claims normal agents cannot review or use those prototypes; only a dragnet/associates composition process can make them useful.
It extends the same claim to an Obsidian prototype and to several dozen similar experiments.
It states that 'appropriate' is a definition only the author can give.
It references transcripts and produced files as the locations of these experiments.
standouts: The strong claim that existing prototypes are valuable yet invalid-by-default and unusable by normal agents.
The assertion that the dragnet/graph execution system is the only legitimate path to extract design value from those prototypes.
The target-agnostic annotation pipeline framing where mode is a lens, not a separate system.
conventions: Follows the noticeboard item YAML frontmatter schema (id, address, type, source, state, title, author_session, channel, links, timestamps, history).
Uses internal address URIs such as board:// and ui://.
Breaks conventional prose style: run-on, informal, stream-of-consciousness writing with repeated clauses.
concerns: The prose is informal and grammatically loose, which may impede automated interpretation.
It makes strong normative claims ('illegal and invalid by default', 'only I can give') without supporting evidence in the file.
It references many external artifacts (prototypes, transcripts, repositories) without concrete identifiers or paths.
It does not provide a concrete schema, registry, or code for the proposed annotation system.
notes: A design note arguing that a target-agnostic annotation pipeline and the dragnet composition system are prerequisites for salvaging Tim's prior agent-built prototypes.
questions: What exactly is the 'dragnet' and what are its 'associates'?
What is the 'graph execution system which is the company'?
What are the exact repository paths or identifiers for the vi-visual-bridge and Obsidian prototypes?
What does 'illegal and invalid by default' mean in technical or policy terms?
What is the intended schema for the 'annotatable surfaces' registry?
How does the mode-as-lens change specific envelope fields?
What is the envisioned step-by-step process for using prototypes via the dragnet?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to comment on a target-agnostic annotation pipeline and point to relevant prior prototypes; the actual content mixes design affirmation, project history, and methodological assertions that the dragnet is the only legitimate way to use those prototypes.
apparent_themes: Mode as a lens rather than a separate system
Authorial authority over what counts as 'appropriate'
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to establish that the proposed annotation system should not ignore Tim's earlier experiments, while also insisting that those experiments cannot be reused directly and must be mediated by a dragnet-based composition process.
novelty: The framing of a large body of prior prototypes as simultaneously valuable and invalid-by-default, requiring a specific composition system to become usable, is unusual.
contribution: This file contributes historical and contextual links between the target-agnostic annotation pipeline idea and prior prototypes, a warning against treating those prototypes as directly reusable, and an assertion that a composition/dragnet process is needed to integrate their intentions.
summary_for_embedding: A noticeboard note by Tim replying to an annotation-pipeline idea, stating that prior agent-built prototypes (vi-visual, Obsidian, dozens more) are tangled and invalid by default and can only be made useful through a dragnet/graph-execution composition system.
intention_for_embedding: To argue that designing the target-agnostic annotation system must proceed via the dragnet and cannot reuse earlier prototypes directly.
